tasks.name: Dismantling of parts from initial taper module

projects.name: Project Mercedes - "6-Sides Tray Taping Machine"
projects.description: A compact 6-Sides inspection tray based taping machine integrated with AI vision inspection capability designed to process packages that doesn't require 3D leads or balls inspection.

milestones.name: Machine body frame and base assembly
Input tray stacker and loader module
Output tray unloader and stacker module
Reject tray unloader and stacker module
AI Vision image capturing and training

tasks.name: Define business case, budget envelope and target completion date
Form project team & assign PM (mechanical, electrical, controls, software, QA)
Capture User Requirements Spec (URS): throughput (UPH), component types & package sizes
Define carrier-tape & pocket specs, reel size, tape width, pitch (EIA-481 / IEC 60286)
Define sealing method (heat-seal vs cold/pressure-seal) and cover-tape type
Define inline functions: counting, missing-component detection, polarity test, vision, laser marking
Define facility / utility constraints (footprint, power, compressed air, ESD, MES/network)
Define FAT / SAT acceptance criteria (throughput, seal integrity, peel force, miss-detect, defect escape)
Develop functional flow & machine layout (infeed → load → seal → inspect → take-up reel)
Select handling mechanism (gravity-fed vs pick-and-place; rotary turret vs swing-arm vacuum head)
Concept for sealing station (seal blades; temperature / pressure / dwell control)
Concept for inspection & marking modules (vision, polarity, laser)
Preliminary control architecture (PLC, HMI, drives, safety)
Build & test proof-of-concept for high-risk mechanisms (pick-up repeatability, seal repeatability)
Preliminary BOM, cost estimate & make-vs-buy decisions
Detailed mechanical 3D CAD & drawings (frame, feeder, sealing, take-up, indexing)
Tolerance stack-up & pocket/sprocket indexing analysis (EIA-481 compliance)
Controls design: PLC I/O list, HMI screens, motion/drive sizing, safety circuit (interlocks, E-stop)
Vision & sensor specification (cameras, lighting, counting fibre sensors)
Laser-marking subsystem spec & safety (enclosure, laser-class compliance)
Finalise complete BOM (mechanical, electrical, pneumatic, controls)
Issue RFQs & select vendors (form, fit, function, cost, lead time)
Place POs for long-lead items (servo drives, cameras, laser marker, PLC)
CNC machining & fabrication of custom parts (frame, brackets, tooling)
Assemble sealing station module (seal head, heaters, take-up reel)
Mechanical integration of all modules onto frame
Electrical wiring & panel integration; continuity / safety checks
Install drives, motors, sensors, cameras, laser marker
Mechanical alignment & indexing calibration (tape pitch / sprocket)
Tune sealing parameters (temperature, pressure, dwell, speed) & verify seal integrity
Tune polarity test & vision inspection (defect detection, pin-1 orientation)
Software debug; alarms, interlocks & safety validation
Prepare FAT protocol & test samples (per URS acceptance criteria)
Verify miss-detect / missing-component capture & reject sorting
Verify vision inspection, polarity & laser-mark quality
Document results; raise & resolve punch list
Disassemble (as needed), crate & prepare for shipment
Connect utilities (power, compressed air, network/MES, ESD ground)
Re-assemble & reconnect modules; verify no transit damage
Repeat key FAT tests under production conditions
Integrate with SMT line / up- & down-stream equipment & MES
Verify throughput, OEE & cycle time at production load
Deliver as-built documentation (drawings, schematics, BOM, PLC code, manuals)
Performance monitoring & data analytics (throughput, downtime, yield)
